steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the verb in the relative clause "which ______ located in Newark, New Jersey." The relative pronoun "which" refers to the singular noun "Newark Liberty International Airport," so the verb must agree with a singular subject and describe a permanent, present-day fact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is': Singular present tense. Agrees with the singular antecedent "which" (= the airport) and fits a permanent geographic fact. ✓
  • Choice B — 'are': Plural present tense. "Which" refers to a single airport, not a plural noun. Number mismatch. ✗
  • Choice C — 'were': Plural past tense. Fails on both number (plural) and tense (past) — the airport's location is a current fact. ✗
  • Choice D — 'have been': Plural present perfect. Still plural, so it disagrees with the singular "which." ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
